description: This course introduces Python as a first programming language. You will master syntax variables control flow functions and basic error handling through short exercises. Learners gain skills to automate tasks parse data and prepare for object-oriented or data-focused tracks. Designed for beginners with no prior coding experience.
features: Python syntax variables functions control flow exercises automation beginner programming

description: Build robust programs using object-oriented design principles in Python. Topics include classes inheritance polymorphism abstract patterns and modules for scalable code. You will apply these skills to APIs domain modeling and larger team projects. Best suited to learners who have completed introductory Python.
features: object oriented classes inheritance polymorphism modules design patterns intermediate programming

description: Develop algorithmic thinking and implement core structures such as arrays trees graphs heaps and hash tables. You will analyze time and space complexity and practice sorting searching and dynamic programming patterns. These skills are essential for technical interviews and efficient software at scale. Target audience includes aspiring engineers with basic programming fluency.
features: algorithms complexity trees graphs sorting searching dynamic programming interviews computer science

description: Learn relational database fundamentals and write practical SQL for reporting and applications. Coverage includes SELECT joins aggregations filtering and introductory schema design. You will be able to extract trustworthy insights from transactional data. Intended for analysts developers and students new to databases.
features: SQL joins aggregations queries database design reporting beginner database

description: Deepen your SQL expertise with window functions CTEs stored routines indexing strategies and query tuning. You will design performant queries for analytics pipelines and production systems. Emphasis is placed on readability maintainability and measurable performance gains. For practitioners who already write everyday SQL.
features: window functions CTE indexing optimization stored procedures intermediate database

description: Survey supervised and unsupervised learning including regression classification clustering and model evaluation. You will train models with scikit-learn interpret metrics and avoid common leakage pitfalls. Practical labs connect theory to tabular and structured datasets. Appropriate for learners with statistics and Python basics.
features: supervised unsupervised regression classification clustering evaluation scikit-learn intermediate machine learning

description: Design and train neural networks using TensorFlow and Keras for vision sequence and tabular problems. Topics span CNNs RNNs regularization transfer learning and experiment tracking. You will deploy prototypes and interpret training dynamics responsibly. Requires solid Python linear algebra and prior ML exposure.
features: TensorFlow Keras CNN RNN neural networks transfer learning advanced deep learning

description: Explore modern natural language processing from tokenization to transformer-based models. You will build sentiment classifiers summarizers and embeddings using industry-standard libraries. Ethical considerations around bias and deployment are woven throughout. Geared toward engineers ready to apply NLP in products.
features: NLP transformers tokenization BERT sentiment embeddings advanced language models

description: Study image classification detection segmentation and classical plus deep vision pipelines. Hands-on work includes OpenCV preprocessing CNN architectures and object detection frameworks. You will connect models to real sensing constraints and evaluation protocols. For advanced learners pursuing vision or robotics applications.
features: OpenCV CNN detection segmentation YOLO classification advanced computer vision

description: Turn raw data into clear compelling visuals using Matplotlib Seaborn and Plotly. You will learn chart selection accessibility color storytelling and dashboard-ready layouts. Exercises emphasize communicating uncertainty and insights to stakeholders. Beginner-friendly with basic spreadsheet or Python comfort helpful.
features: Matplotlib Seaborn Plotly charts dashboards storytelling beginner data visualization

description: Strengthen inferential statistics for data-driven decisions including distributions confidence intervals and hypothesis tests. You will connect math intuition to A/B tests and modeling assumptions. Case studies reflect analytics and experimentation in industry. Ideal for analysts and ML students strengthening quantitative foundations.
features: statistics hypothesis testing distributions inference confidence intervals intermediate mathematics

description: Master vectors matrices eigenvalues and decompositions used throughout ML and graphics. Lessons bridge geometric intuition with numerical computation in Python. You will be prepared for optimization dimensionality reduction and deep learning theory. Suited to learners who have completed introductory calculus or programming.
features: linear algebra matrices eigenvectors decomposition ML foundations intermediate mathematics

description: Focus on derivatives integrals and multivariate concepts that underpin optimization and neural networks. Applications include gradients backpropagation intuition and basic convexity ideas. Worked examples tie calculus to loss surfaces and training behavior. For students moving from algebra into ML-heavy curricula.
features: calculus derivatives gradients optimization backpropagation multivariate intermediate mathematics

description: Build probabilistic models and reasoning with discrete and continuous distributions Bayes rule and simulation. You will interpret uncertainty quantify beliefs and relate topics to modern Bayesian ML. Exercises reinforce decision-making under incomplete information. Target audience includes data scientists and quantitative analysts.
features: probability Bayes distributions simulation uncertainty inference intermediate mathematics

description: Collect structured data from the web responsibly using HTTP clients HTML parsing and browser automation basics. You will handle pagination rate limits and simple cleaning pipelines. Legal and ethical scraping practices are highlighted throughout. Beginners with Python fundamentals will progress quickly.
features: BeautifulSoup Selenium requests parsing HTML extraction beginner programming

description: Build secure maintainable web services with Flask including routing templates blueprints and REST patterns. You will integrate authentication configuration and deployment to common hosts. Projects mirror small production APIs and content sites. Intended for developers expanding from Python scripts into full web stacks.
features: Flask REST templates authentication deployment APIs intermediate web development

description: Engineer enterprise-style APIs with Django REST Framework serializers permissions and versioning. Topics include ORM performance testing pagination and integration with front-end clients. You will practice patterns used in large Python codebases. Prior Django or strong web fundamentals are recommended.
features: Django REST serializers ORM authentication testing advanced web development

description: Containerize applications and orchestrate them on Kubernetes with services ingress and observability hooks. Labs cover images multi-stage builds Helm basics and CI-friendly workflows. You will improve reliability and portability across environments. For engineers modernizing delivery platforms.
features: Docker Kubernetes containers orchestration microservices CI CD advanced devops

description: Learn distributed version control workflows branching merging rebasing and collaborative pull requests. You will configure repositories resolve conflicts and adopt team conventions safely. Real-world scenarios mirror open-source and commercial practices. No prior VCS experience required.
features: Git branching merge pull requests collaboration version control beginner tools

description: Architect resilient workloads on AWS using compute storage networking and serverless building blocks. You will design for scalability cost observability and least-privilege security. Case studies map common patterns to well-architected guidance. Best for practitioners ready to own cloud infrastructure decisions.
features: AWS EC2 S3 Lambda RDS scalability cloud architecture advanced cloud

description: Create executive-ready dashboards in Power BI with data modeling DAX and refresh automation. You will connect to diverse sources define KPIs and publish governed reports. Storytelling and layout best practices are emphasized. Geared toward analysts translating business questions into interactive insights.
features: Power BI DAX dashboards KPIs business intelligence intermediate data visualization

description: Learn Tableau fundamentals for exploratory analysis and polished dashboards. Skills include calculated fields parameters maps and sharing to Tableau Server or Cloud. Activities reinforce speed to insight for stakeholders. Suitable for newcomers to visual analytics.
features: Tableau charts dashboards calculated fields analytics beginner data visualization

description: Advance from spreadsheets to repeatable analysis using pivot tables lookups and charting in Excel. You will clean messy data document assumptions and present findings clearly. Productivity shortcuts and error-checking habits are included. Designed for professionals who rely on Excel daily.
features: Excel pivot tables analysis charts data cleaning beginner data visualization

description: Program effectively in R for statistics visualization and tidy data workflows with dplyr and ggplot2. You will import transform and communicate results reproducibly. Projects mirror academic and industry analytics pipelines. Assumes prior statistics coursework or concurrent study.
features: R ggplot2 dplyr tidyverse statistics visualization intermediate programming

description: Model temporal data with classical and modern approaches including ARIMA Prophet and sequence models. You will handle seasonality missing values and backtesting rigorously. Use cases span demand planning operations and finance. Requires strong statistics and ML foundations.
features: ARIMA Prophet LSTM forecasting seasonality trends advanced machine learning

description: Study collaborative filtering matrix factorization hybrid recommenders and evaluation offline versus online. You will implement ranking metrics cold-start mitigations and responsible personalization. Labs reference large sparse interaction datasets. For ML engineers focusing on engagement and discovery products.
features: collaborative filtering matrix factorization ranking evaluation advanced machine learning

description: Understand agents environments policies and value methods with hands-on Gym-style experiments. Topics include Q-learning policy gradients and exploration trade-offs. You will connect theory to robotics games and decision systems. Demanding course for learners with calculus probability and deep learning maturity.
features: reinforcement learning Q-learning policy gradient Gym rewards advanced machine learning

description: Examine fairness accountability transparency and privacy in machine learning systems. Case studies cover dataset bias mitigation documentation and stakeholder communication. You will draft responsible AI checklists aligned to emerging regulation. Valuable for builders and leaders shipping AI features.
features: fairness bias privacy transparency responsible regulation intermediate ethics

description: Process large datasets with Apache Spark including DataFrames SQL APIs and structured streaming. You will reason about partitions shuffles and cluster tuning on batch and near-real-time workloads. Integrations with data lakes and warehouses are discussed. For engineers scaling beyond single-machine Python.
features: Spark Hadoop streaming DataFrames scalability cluster advanced big data

description: Operationalize models with reproducible training CI/CD for ML monitoring and safe rollouts. Tools include container registries feature stores and experiment tracking patterns. You will align data science and platform teams for reliable production inference. Assumes prior ML and basic DevOps exposure.
features: MLOps deployment monitoring MLflow Docker CI CD pipelines advanced mlops
